title: ConvertKit vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool suits your business best.
h1: ConvertKit vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketing
intro: ConvertKit and Drip are both established email marketing platforms founded in 2013, but they cater to different audiences. ConvertKit targets creators and content builders with a freemium model, while Drip focuses on e-commerce businesses with paid-only pricing. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right platform for your specific needs.
choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course creator needing affordability and landing pages
You want to start with a free tier before committing to paid features
You prefer creator-focused tools with affiliate program benefits (30% recurring)
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep store integration and revenue tracking
You require SMS marketing capabilities alongside email
You want onsite campaigns and advanced segmentation for customer behavior

faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit is better for creators and content creators due to its free tier and creator-focused features. Drip is better for e-commerce businesses that need advanced tracking, SMS, and store integration. Both have identical G2 ratings of 4.4/5.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Drip?
faq.a: ConvertKit is more affordable overall. It offers a free tier up to 1,000 subscribers and starts at $29/month for paid plans. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious users, ConvertKit is the clear winner.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list exports, making migration possible. However, you may lose some platform-specific features like Drip's e-commerce tracking or ConvertKit's landing pages. Plan your switch carefully and test automations after migration.
